NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. On the instrument of Gath. A Psalm of David. O LORD, our Lord, How excellent is Your name in all the earth, Who have set Your glory above the heavens!
NKJV.2: Out of the mouth of babes and nursing infants You have ordained strength, Because of Your enemies, That You may silence the enemy and the avenger.
NKJV.3: When I consider Your heavens, the work of Your fingers, The moon and the stars, which You have ordained,
NKJV.4: What is man that You are mindful of him, And the son of man that You visit him?
NKJV.5: For You have made him a little lower than the angels, And You have crowned him with glory and honor.
NKJV.6: You have made him to have dominion over the works of Your hands; You have put all things under his feet,
NKJV.7: All sheep and oxen— Even the beasts of the field,
NKJV.8: The birds of the air, And the fish of the sea That pass through the paths of the seas.
NKJV.9: O LORD, our Lord, How excellent is Your name in all the earth!

KJV.3: When I consider<S>7200</S> thy heavens<S>8064</S>, the work<S>4639</S> of thy fingers<S>676</S>, the moon<S>3394</S> and the stars<S>3556</S>, which thou hast ordained<S>3559</S>;

KJV.5: For thou hast made him a little<S>4592</S> lower<S>2637</S> than the angels<S>430</S>, and hast crowned<S>5849</S> him with glory<S>3519</S> and honour<S>1926</S>.
KJV.6: Thou madest him to have dominion<S>4910</S> over the works<S>4639</S> of thy hands<S>3027</S>; thou hast put<S>7896</S> all things under his feet<S>7272</S>:
KJV.7: All sheep<S>6792</S> and oxen<S>504</S>, yea, and the beasts<S>929</S> of the field<S>7704</S>; <sup>All: Heb. Flocks and oxen all of them</sup>
KJV.8: The fowl<S>6833</S> of the air<S>8064</S>, and the fish<S>1709</S> of the sea<S>3220</S>, and whatsoever passeth through<S>5674</S> the paths<S>734</S> of the seas<S>3220</S>.

ESV.1: O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth! You have set your glory above the heavens.
ESV.2: Out of the mouth of babies and infants, you have established strength because of your foes, to still the enemy and the avenger.
ESV.3: When I look at your heavens, the work of your fingers, the moon and the stars, which you have set in place,
ESV.4: what is man that you are mindful of him, and the son of man that you care for him?
ESV.5: Yet you have made him a little lower than the heavenly beings  and crowned him with glory and honor.
ESV.6: You have given him dominion over the works of your hands; you have put all things under his feet,
ESV.7: all sheep and oxen, and also the beasts of the field,
ESV.8: the birds of the heavens, and the fish of the sea, whatever passes along the paths of the seas.
ESV.9: O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth!

NASB.1: For the choir director; on the Gittith. A Psalm of David. O LORD, our Lord, How majestic is Your name in all the earth, Who have displayed Your splendor above the heavens!
NASB.2: From the mouth of infants and nursing babes You have established strength Because of Your adversaries, To make the enemy and the revengeful cease.
NASB.3: When I consider Your heavens, the work of Your fingers, The moon and the stars, which You have ordained;
NASB.4: What is man that You take thought of him, And the son of man that You care for him?
NASB.5: Yet You have made him a little lower than God, And You crown him with glory and majesty!
NASB.6: You make him to rule over the works of Your hands; You have put all things under his feet,
NASB.7: All sheep and oxen, And also the beasts of the field,
NASB.8: The birds of the heavens and the fish of the sea, Whatever passes through the paths of the seas.
NASB.9: O LORD, our Lord, How majestic is Your name in all the earth!

NLT.1:
O LORD, our Lord, your majestic name fills the earth!
Your glory is higher than the heavens.
NLT.2:
You have taught children and infants
to tell of your strength,
silencing your enemies
and all who oppose you.
NLT.3:
When I look at the night sky and see the work of your fingers —
the moon and the stars you set in place —
NLT.4:
what are mere mortals that you should think about them,
human beings that you should care for them?
NLT.5:
Yet you made them only a little lower than God
and crowned them with glory and honor.
NLT.6:
You gave them charge of everything you made,
putting all things under their authority —
NLT.7:
the flocks and the herds
and all the wild animals,
NLT.8:
the birds in the sky, the fish in the sea,
and everything that swims the ocean currents.
NLT.9: O LORD, our Lord, your majestic name fills the earth!

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician; on an instrument of Gath. A Psalm by David. Yahweh, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth, who has set your glory above the heavens!
WEB.2: From the lips of babes and infants you have established strength, because of your adversaries, that you might silence the enemy and the avenger.
WEB.3: When I consider your heavens, the work of your fingers, the moon and the stars, which you have ordained;
WEB.4: what is man, that you think of him? What is the son of man, that you care for him?
WEB.5: For you have made him a little lower than God,  and crowned him with glory and honor.
WEB.6: You make him ruler over the works of your hands. You have put all things under his feet:
WEB.7: All sheep and cattle, yes, and the animals of the field,
WEB.8: The birds of the sky, the fish of the sea, and whatever passes through the paths of the seas.
WEB.9: Yahweh, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth!

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to a Philistine lute [or perhaps to a particular Hittite tune]. A Psalm of David. O LORD, our Lord,
How majestic and glorious and excellent is Your name in all the earth!
You have displayed Your splendor above the heavens.
AMP.2:
Out of the mouths of infants and nursing babes You have established strength
Because of Your adversaries,
That You might silence the enemy and make the revengeful cease.
AMP.3:
When I see and consider Your heavens, the work of Your fingers,
The moon and the stars, which You have established,
AMP.4:
What is man that You are mindful of him,
And the son of [earthborn] man that You care for him?
AMP.5:
Yet You have made him a little lower than God,
And You have crowned him with glory and honor.
AMP.6:
You made him to have dominion over the works of Your hands;
You have put all things under his feet,
AMP.7:
All sheep and oxen,
And also the beasts of the field,
AMP.8:
The birds of the air, and the fish of the sea,
Whatever passes through the paths of the seas.
AMP.9:
O LORD, our Lord,
How majestic and glorious and excellent is Your name in all the earth!

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God, brilliant Lord,
yours is a household name.
MSG.2:
Nursing infants gurgle choruses about you;
toddlers shout the songs
That drown out enemy talk,
and silence atheist babble.
MSG.3:
I look up at your macro-skies, dark and enormous,
your handmade sky-jewelry,
Moon and stars mounted in their settings.
MSG.4:
Then I look at my micro-self and wonder,
Why do you bother with us?
Why take a second look our way?
MSG.5:
Yet we've so narrowly missed being gods,
bright with Eden's dawn light.
MSG.6:
You put us in charge of your handcrafted world,
repeated to us your Genesis-charge,
MSG.7:
Made us lords of sheep and cattle,
even animals out in the wild,
MSG.8:
Birds flying and fish swimming,
whales singing in the ocean deeps.
MSG.9:
God, brilliant Lord,
your name echoes around the world.

NIV.1:
Psalm 8
For the director of music. According to gittith. A psalm of David.
O Lord, our Lord,
how majestic is your name in all the earth!
You have set your glory
above the heavens.
NIV.2:
From the lips of children and infants
you have ordained praise
because of your enemies,
to silence the foe and the avenger.
NIV.3:
When I consider your heavens,
the work of your fingers,
the moon and the stars,
which you have set in place,
NIV.4:
what is man that you are mindful of him,
the son of man that you care for him?
NIV.5:
You made him a little lower than the heavenly beings
and crowned him with glory and honor.
NIV.6:
You made him ruler over the works of your hands;
you put everything under his feet:
NIV.7:
all flocks and herds,
and the beasts of the field,
NIV.8:
the birds of the air,
and the fish of the sea,
all that swim the paths of the seas.
NIV.9:
O Lord, our Lord,
how majestic is your name in all the earth!

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD, our Sovereign,
how majestic is your name in all the earth!
You have set your glory above the heavens.
NRSVCE.2:
Out of the mouths of babes and infants
you have founded a bulwark because of your foes,
to silence the enemy and the avenger.
NRSVCE.3:
When I look at your heavens, the work of your fingers,
the moon and the stars that you have established;
NRSVCE.4:
what are human beings that you are mindful of them,
mortals that you care for them?
NRSVCE.5:
Yet you have made them a little lower than God,
and crowned them with glory and honor.
NRSVCE.6:
You have given them dominion over the works of your hands;
you have put all things under their feet,
NRSVCE.7:
all sheep and oxen,
and also the beasts of the field,
NRSVCE.8:
the birds of the air, and the fish of the sea,
whatever passes along the paths of the seas.
NRSVCE.9:
O LORD, our Sovereign,
how majestic is your name in all the earth!

NET.1: O LORD, our Lord, how magnificent is your reputation throughout the earth! You reveal your majesty in the heavens above!
NET.2: From the mouths of children and nursing babies you have ordained praise on account of your adversaries, so that you might put an end to the vindictive enemy.
NET.3: When I look up at the heavens, which your fingers made, and see the moon and the stars, which you set in place,
NET.4: Of what importance is the human race, that you should notice them? Of what importance is mankind, that you should pay attention to them,
NET.5: and make them a little less than the heavenly beings? You grant mankind honor and majesty;
NET.6: you appoint them to rule over your creation; you have placed everything under their authority,
NET.7: including all the sheep and cattle, as well as the wild animals,
NET.8: the birds in the sky, the fish in the sea and everything that moves through the currents of the seas.
NET.9: O LORD, our Lord, how magnificent is your reputation throughout the earth!
